topic: Theme 3: Immigration and the Spanish-speaking Multicultural Society
subject: spanish

items.q: Which preposition expresses 'for' as duration (e.g., 'for two hours')?
items.why: Students choose para because it's the default 'for'. POR is used for duration/extent of time. Para 'for two hours' would suggest a deadline ('to be ready BY two hours' time').

items.q: What is the Spanish word for 'mobile phone' (in Spain)?
items.misconception: El celular (which is used in Latin America).
items.why: Vocabulary varies by region. Spain: móvil, ordenador (computer), coche (car). Latin America: celular, computadora, carro/auto. Students mix these. Examiners accept both, but consistency matters. Móvil is most common in GCSE/A-level.

items.q: How do you say 'I love films' in Spanish?
items.why: Students translate 'amar' (which is for people/love) or forget gustar/encantar structure. ENCANTAR works like gustar - backwards. Subject = películas (plural), so verb = ENCANTAN. 'Me encanta el cine' (singular subject) vs 'me encantan las películas' (plural).

items.q: Which conjunction can take the subjunctive?
items.why: It's aunque. “porque” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: When did Spain become a net immigration country?
items.why: Correct answer: 1990s. “1980s” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many years of social integration are required for 'arraigo social' in Spain?
items.why: The real figure is 3 years. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
